prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
Verily, in the third moon’s quarter, the House of Softwares did conjure forth a revenue most strange, measured not in bushels nor hogsheads, but in ethereal tokens called “ARR.”  
The scribes speak of “user engagement,” which I take to mean the peasants are oft betrothed to their glowing scrying stones, though no banns are read.  
A council of elders, known as “the Board,” did gather via enchanted mirrors to debate the fate of the “churn rate,” a malady that seems to afflict only numbers and not livestock.  
Lastly, I am bid to record that the “pipeline” is robust, though I see no water nor grain flowing, only the promise of future coinage from lands as distant as the fabled realm of LinkedIn.
